summary: Phase 2 (IPv6 source rotation) testing complete. Added 9 new IPv6 backend tests (TestIPv6 class) to /app/backend/tests/test_resilience_backend.py. Full pytest suite: 41/41 PASS in 55.27s (32 regression from Phase 1.5 + 9 new). Verified: GET /api/system/ipv6 (auth gated, returns unavailable/false/can_rotate=false in preview env with correct shape and 'no global IPv6 /64 subnet found' reason), POST /api/system/ipv6/reprobe (auth gated, returns same capability dict), POST /api/tests with ipv6_rotation=true & ipv6_count=500 returns ipv6_mode='simulation' / ipv6_pool_size=0 in preview env, default request returns ipv6_mode='off' / pool=0, ipv6_rotation=true with ipv6_count=0 stays 'off', ipv6_count=2001 rejected with 422 by Pydantic, GET /api/tests and /api/tests/{id} both include ipv6_pool_size & ipv6_mode. Frontend live-tested on https://check-enhance-1.preview.emergentagent.com against admin@resilience.lab: [04] IPV6_SOURCE_ROTATION panel renders with badge '○ UNAVAILABLE', ipv6-warning shows '⊘ IPV6 NOT DETECTED' + reason + DEPLOYMENT.md hint, ipv6-toggle reveals form-ipv6-count-slider + form-ipv6-count-input, launch-summary shows 'IPV6_POOL 500 addrs'. TestRun page shows 'test-ipv6-info' = 'IPV6: simulation' for a simulation test. No regressions in Phase 1/1.5 features. All criteria from review_request satisfied.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py keeps growing (~1040 lines now) — past the 700-line soft threshold. Suggest splitting into modules (auth, projects, verify, tests, k6_runner, system, ipv6) before Phase 3 to keep diff review feasible.

ipv6_rotator.add_addresses uses sequential `ip -6 addr add` per address with 1.0s timeout — for the max pool of 2000 addresses this can take ~minutes serially. Consider batched `ip -batch -` (stdin) or parallel asyncio.gather with bounded semaphore on live VPS deploy.

create_test allocates the IPv6 pool synchronously inside the request path before scheduling k6 — on live mode a 2000-address pool blocks the HTTP response for as long as kernel binding takes. Move to background task and surface progressive status via the test document.
IPV6_CAPABILITY is mutated globally without a lock; concurrent reprobes (admin spam) could race. Cheap fix: wrap in asyncio.Lock or only allow one reprobe at a time.
Pool generation uses secrets.randbelow inside a Python set loop — fine for 2000 IPs but for /64 collision risk is essentially zero, so a simple range comprehension would be faster. Non-blocking.

Cleanup of added addrs happens in run_k6_subprocess finally block — if backend crashes between add_addresses and process spawn, addresses leak on the iface. Track ipv6_pool in a global set and clean on startup as a safety net.
/api/system/ipv6/reprobe has no rate-limit; could be DoS'd to spawn many `ip -6 addr add/del` subprocesses. Add simple per-user throttle.

Carry-over from iteration_2: CORS_ORIGINS='*' with allow_credentials=True; no login brute-force lockout; first /api/system/live after restart shows cpu_avg=0; <span> inside <option> hydration warning on NewTest project dropdown.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@resilience.lab / admin123 (seeded). Test users auto-registered with TEST_ prefix.
seed_data_creation: Tests create + force-verify example.com project via pymongo (db.projects.updateOne {verified: true}) and create several TEST_-prefixed smoke/burst/mixed/ipv6 tests. UI verification reused a TEST_UI ipv6 simulation test from pytest run (and would create one if absent). Safe to delete.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_resilience_backend.py = 41 tests across 8 classes (TestAuth, TestProjects, TestStats, TestSystemEndpoints, TestVerifyMethods, TestPhase15Scenarios, TestIPv6, TestTestRunner). Run with: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_resilience_backend.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Suite ~55-70s (includes one real k6 smoke). Preview env IPV6_CAPABILITY is permanently {available:false, mode:'unavailable'} — to test 'live' mode the dashboard must be deployed to a real VPS with root + /64 (see /app/DEPLOYMENT.md). The /api/tests POST returns ipv6_pool_size & ipv6_mode and persists them; UI surfaces them on /tests/:id via data-testid='test-ipv6-info'. Hardware caps unchanged: 28000 RPS / 4000 VUs.
rca of the issue: n/a — Phase 2 IPv6 source rotation feature fully verified; graceful fallback to simulation mode works correctly in the restricted preview environment.
